what_it_does: Acceptance test for the backend composed address-help surface. It builds a fresh Suite with a real NodeRegistry and a temporary FsStore, calls Suite.address_help on five representative ui:// addresses, and asserts bundle structure, leg presence flags, and degrade/fail-loud behavior.
observations: Top-level script-style test with a global PASS counter and a check() helper that raises SystemExit on failure.
Uses real discovered nodes and the real corpus registry; fixtures are live addresses rather than hardcoded prose expectations.
Covers five branches: full three-leg bundle (A), thin how-to-use (B), synthetic no-code how-to-change (C), unregistered (D), and malformed/fail-loud (E).
Injects a synthetic no-code fixture into su.UI_REGISTRY because the real corpus no longer contains a registered address with an empty code scope.
Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 for inbox-hygiene parity with sibling suites.
Creates a fresh Suite per run via fresh_suite() using tempfile.mkdtemp.
Inline comments extensively explain design decisions, corpus evolution (GC5, connect+unify), and why the synthetic fixture is needed.
standouts: Synthetic fixture injection to preserve a regression guard for a shape that no longer exists in the real corpus.
Very long, narrative docstring and comments that read like a design ledger as much as a test.
Fail-loud malformed-address test that expects an exception rather than a silent empty bundle.
conventions: Uses fresh_suite() helper to isolate state.
Uses check() helper with global PASS counter.
Fixtures are real corpus addresses chosen for structural shape.
Inline comments document rationale and known corpus evolution.
concerns: check() raises SystemExit(1) instead of a test-framework assertion, which can abort the whole run.
MALFORMED test catches any Exception, not the specific S0 grammar-gate exception, so it could pass for unrelated failures.
Synthetic mutation of su.UI_REGISTRY is local to the Suite instance but depends on internal data structure shape.
Temp directory created by tempfile.mkdtemp is not explicitly cleaned up.
notes: Backend acceptance test for the address-help surface that verifies full bundles and honest degrade paths.
questions: What is the exact schema of legs_present and how_to_change returned by Suite.address_help?
What exception type does the S0 grammar gate raise for malformed addresses?
How is the GET /api/address-help bridge route tested, given this file has no HTTP harness?
What do G-53, G-61/GC1, GC5, X9, and X14 refer to in the comments?
Will the RICH and THIN fixtures remain stable as the corpus evolves?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file does exactly what the docstring describes—exercises Suite.address_help across full, degraded, and malformed cases.
apparent_themes: Degrade-clean UX: honest absence beats fabricated content.
Regression guard preservation through synthetic fixtures.
intention_signals: To lock down the contract of Suite.address_help so the address-help surface never crashes, fabricates, or silently degrades, and to document why a synthetic fixture is needed after corpus improvements removed the real no-code case.
novelty: The synthetic no-code fixture injection lets the test keep proving a defensive property even though the real corpus no longer contains a registered address with an empty code scope.
contribution: A behavioral contract test for Suite.address_help covering full-panel, thin-howto, no-code, unregistered, and malformed cases, plus a reusable pattern for injecting synthetic fixtures to preserve regression guards when corpus evolution eliminates the natural triggering example.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test that exercises Suite.address_help on real and synthetic ui:// addresses to verify full three-leg bundles and clean degrade paths for thin, no-code, unregistered, and malformed inputs.
intention_for_embedding: To regression-guard the backend address-help surface contract and preserve a no-code degrade test after corpus improvements removed the real fixture.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that increments PASS on success or raises SystemExit on failure.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) maintains a global PASS counter and prints PASS/FAIL per assertion; on failure it immediately exits the process with code 1, giving the suite a simple pass/fail signal.

symbols.does: Constructs a fresh Suite with a temporary FsStore and a NodeRegistry discovered from the nodes directory.
symbols.description: fresh_suite() creates an isolated Suite instance for each test run by combining a newly discovered NodeRegistry with a temporary FsStore, ensuring the acceptance test operates against real discovered nodes without persisting state.
